findings.title: FastMCP API name unconfirmed
findings.description: Design assumes mcp_obj.streamable_http_app() exists. If FastMCP exposes it under a different name (e.g. http_app, asgi_app, sse_app, _streamable), implementation breaks at import.
findings.mitigation: Spike during Step 5: dir(mcp) then mount under whatever name resolves. Documented as Task #1 in design §3.

findings.title: /healthz performs tool registration as side effect of import
findings.description: Importing homelab_mcp.server on first /healthz request registers all 133 tools. Cold start latency spike on the FIRST request rather than at uvicorn boot.
findings.mitigation: create_app() now imports server at construction time (before uvicorn.serve). Verify by adding a startup-only timer to the test file and confirming the FIRST /healthz is fast.

findings.title: Auth bypass on Streamable HTTP sub-paths
findings.description: Middleware whitelists exact paths /healthz and /metrics. If FastMCP mounts at /mcp and uses sub-paths like /mcp/sse for SSE, those still hit the middleware. Good. But if the streamable app has its OWN /healthz that we're proxying, the whitelist could leak. Verify FastMCP's mounted paths.
findings.mitigation: Test plan covers it (T-A6 hits /mcp/ and asserts 401). Also: middleware uses == not startswith() so /mcphax can't bypass.

findings.title: Bearer-token comparison is non-constant-time
findings.description: `hdr != f"Bearer {auth_token}"` is a string compare, vulnerable to timing attacks in theory. For homelab env with single user, tolerable; for the work env (Phase 4) we MUST use hmac.compare_digest.
findings.mitigation: Phase 1: accept the risk (homelab is behind SSH today). Add a TODO comment in code citing this finding so Phase 3 hardens before public exposure.

findings.title: Tool count via private attribute
findings.description: _tool_count() reads mcp._tools (private). FastMCP could rename in a minor bump and silently break healthz semantics — _tool_count returns 0 → up=0 → K8s flaps the pod. Fail-safe: degrade healthz?
findings.mitigation: Function tries 3 access patterns and falls through to 0. Healthz still returns 200 (just with tools=0). Liveness probe should NOT key off tool count — it keys off HTTP 200. up gauge correctly flips to 0 → alerting catches it. Acceptable.

findings.title: Image will not start if FastMCP import fails
findings.description: If FastMCP version drift breaks streamable_http_app(), uvicorn boot crashes. mcpo path was crash-resistant because mcpo subprocess-isolated FastMCP failures.
findings.mitigation: Acceptable trade — image is pinned to a sha tag, so FastMCP version is frozen per image. Bumping mcp[cli] requires a new image build & test cycle. Add a minimal smoke step to GHCR build CI: docker run + curl /healthz, fail the build if 200 not returned.

findings.title: Tests could pass for the wrong reason
findings.description: test_unauth_mcp_is_401_when_token_set posts to /mcp/ with empty body. Could a 422 (validation) accidentally match? No — assert is == 401, not !=200. But if middleware order shifts (FastAPI runs route validation before middleware?), test could pass with wrong root cause.
findings.mitigation: Add a positive companion: test that WITH a token AND empty body, response is NOT 401 (i.e. middleware passed). Drop in test #6.

findings.title: Spec/design inconsistency: docs_url=None vs Phase 2 swagger
findings.description: design.md sets docs_url=None for Phase 1. Phase 2 spec will need to flip it on. Make sure the code comment cross-refs the Phase 2 milestone so future maintainer doesn't think it was an oversight.
findings.mitigation: Comment already in design §2.1 ('Phase 2 will enable Swagger'). Mirror the same comment in code.

findings.title: Self-approval: tests run against in-process app, not the deployed image
findings.description: All A1-A11 tests use ASGITransport in-process. They don't prove the Dockerfile works. ENTRYPOINT could be wrong, image could miss uvicorn (e.g., dep added to pyproject but [standard] extra missing in pip install command), and tests still pass.
findings.mitigation: Add T-A9 manual verification in Step 5: docker build + docker run + curl /healthz. NOT optional. Document the curl command verbatim in build-result.json. (Same RC-15 lesson: in-process tests are not enough; need a real deployed integration check.)

findings.title: Gate fabrication: 'tests still pass' without specifying which
findings.description: MP-9 says 'all existing tests still pass' but doesn't pin a list. If something is removed and pytest collects fewer tests, it could pass trivially.
findings.mitigation: build-result.json will record exact pytest collected/passed counts before AND after the change. Reduction in count is a regression.

findings.title: Two parallel image entrypoints in flight
findings.description: Dockerfile changes ENTRYPOINT but Dockerfile.domain (per-domain images) is unchanged. Will per-domain images still work? Are they still using mcpo?
findings.mitigation: Spec lists Dockerfile.domain as 'unchanged' explicitly. Phase 2 (out of scope) addresses the per-domain images. Add a paragraph to design noting that per-domain images stay on the old entrypoint to avoid scope creep.

findings.title: User-capability: SSH path proves end-to-end?
findings.description: Spec MP-6 says stdio entrypoint unchanged. But that's verified by running 'docker run --entrypoint homelab-mcp ...' which is what the host wrapper does. Is it ALSO true that 'docker run' (default ENTRYPOINT, now homelab-mcp-http) STARTS uvicorn and serves /healthz? The user-capability sentence requires both paths simultaneously.
findings.mitigation: Add explicit dual smoke test in Step 7 (Build+Test): (a) docker run -p 8080:8080 → curl /healthz returns 200; (b) docker run --entrypoint homelab-mcp -i ... → JSON-RPC initialize returns serverInfo.name=homelab. BOTH must pass.
